title: AWS Bedrock Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using AWS Bedrock for AI model deployment and management.

commands.description: Enables streaming mode for the GPT-4o model
commands.scenario: When you need real-time responses from the AI model in a chatbot application

commands.description: Sets the model's response randomness to low for deterministic outputs
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in a response
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on processing costs

commands.description: Specifies the AWS region for Bedrock operations
commands.scenario: When deploying models closer to your user base to reduce latency

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically
commands.scenario: When solving complex engineering problems that require logical reasoning

commands.description: Sets a custom endpoint URL for API requests
commands.scenario: When using a specific endpoint for compliance or testing purposes

commands.description: Instructs the model to format responses with bullet points
commands.scenario: When generating reports or documentation that require clear formatting

commands.description: Uses a specific AWS CLI profile for authentication
commands.scenario: When managing multiple AWS accounts and needing to switch contexts

commands.description: Prompts the model to include examples in its responses
commands.scenario: When teaching new concepts or explaining complex ideas

commands.description: Enables verbose output for debugging purposes
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting API requests or model behavior

commands.description: Instructs the model to provide a summary of previous content
commands.scenario: When needing a quick overview of lengthy discussions or documents

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to avoid hanging operations
commands.scenario: When ensuring API requests do not exceed a certain duration

commands.description: Prompts the model to simplify explanations for beginners
commands.scenario: When writing educational content or onboarding new team members

commands.description: Simulates a command without making actual changes
commands.scenario: When testing configuration changes before applying them

commands.description: Use stop sequences to prevent the model from hallucinating additional content or continuing past a specific logical boundary.
Adjust Nucleus Sampling to control the diversity of the output by selecting the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds the threshold.
Implement Few-Shot prompting by providing 2-3 input/output pairs within the prompt to significantly improve pattern recognition and output format adherence.
Use the 'fileb://' prefix in the AWS CLI to pass complex, multi-line JSON payloads from a local file instead of escaping strings in the terminal.

commands.command: Guardrail Implementation: 'applyGuardrail' via Bedrock Agent API
commands.description: Integrate Amazon Bedrock Guardrails to filter PII (Personally Identifiable Information) and toxic content at the inference layer before the response reaches the user.
Adjust top_k parameter to control the diversity of the sampling pool, specifically useful for Claude models to balance creativity and coherence.
Use this CLI command to audit and manage dedicated throughput capacity for high-traffic production workloads to avoid throttling.
Leverage XML-style delimiters to prevent prompt injection and help Claude-family models distinguish between instructions and data.
Define custom action groups using OpenAPI schemas to enable Bedrock Agents to call specific Lambda functions or external APIs.
Fine-tune output variety by penalizing tokens based on their existing frequency or presence in the generated text to reduce repetitive loops.
